chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الرَّجُلِ يَأْخُذُ الْحَجَّةَ فَلَا تَكْفِيهِ أَوْ يَأْخُذُهَا فَيَدْفَعُهَا إِلَى غَيْرِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 63 – The man takes the (responsibility of performing) the Hajj but is not (paid) sufficienty, or he takes it and hands it over to someone else

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/222)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/222)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) رَجُلٌ أُوصِيَ بِحَجَّةٍ فَلَمْ تَكْفِهِ قَالَ فَيُقَدِّمُهَا حَتَّى يُحَجَّ دُونَ الْوَقْتِ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Mohsan Bin Ahmad, from Aban, from Umar Bin Yazeed who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘A man bequeaths with a Hajj, but he does not (pay) sufficiently for it’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So he should proceed until he performs Hajj besides the juncture’.
en.sarwar: 3. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhsin ibn Ahmad from Aban from ‘Imran ibn Yazid who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who says in his will to perform al-Hajj for him but the amount is not sufficient. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is performed from nearby before Miqat (place for assuming the state of Ihram).”’
